AT0051.title: Risk Email Accounts
AT0051.description: From a defense perspective against gray/black markets, risk email accounts are email accounts that may be exploited by criminal actors. They mainly fall into two categories: first, large numbers of legitimate email accounts obtained through malicious registration, fraud, and cyberattacks, used for illegal activities such as sending spam, online fraud, and data breaches; second, temporary email accounts (disposable emails) that can be quickly obtained without real-name registration and are heavily used by criminal actors for batch registration of fake accounts and bypassing email verification.

references.title: Black Market Big Data: Malicious Email Accounts See New Surge
